texto: Doesn't it seem that it is always by chance that these things happen, or is it because of the generosity of a shared knowledge of suffering that allows for it?
explicacao: Pergunta retórica de David Gianadda que provoca temas ligados a sabedoria. Ao questionar “Doesn't it seem that it is always by chance that these things happen, or is it because of the gen…”, o texto convida o leitor a revisar crenças sobre sabedoria, suffering, suffering-of-humanity, com foco em suffering-of-humanity e war.

texto: We too had branched off from the people who had come before us, a family that made the family that made us, and we were shining in the darkness and casting our light all around us.
explicacao: Citação de David Gianadda sobre temas ligados a familia. “We too had branched off from the people who had come before us, a family that made the family tha…” articula familia, family, generations em registro de familia, com relevância para contextos como reflexao-noturna e generations.

texto: The birds are literal representations of the witnesses of those ordinary and big moments, but they are also metaphors for time itself, for the passing of time. It occurred to me, many years after I had been here, thinking about this idea, that every moment we have with one another is really our only moment, and because of that our every moment could potentially be a goodbye, so we have to notice and notice and notice.
explicacao: Orientação direta de David Gianadda sobre passagem do tempo e prioridades. A afirmação “The birds are literal representations of the witnesses of those ordinary and big moments, but the…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a tempo, metaphor, notice, útil em momentos de termino e notice.

It occurred to me, many years after I had been here, thinking about this idea, that every moment we have with one another is really our only moment, and because of that our every moment could potentially be a goodbye, so we have to notice and notice and notice.
